description: Gather reference context for RestructureCodebaseAgent - understands agent framework, token budgeting, context management, and LLM integration

specs.reasoning: Defines Agent trait and execution loop that RestructureCodebaseAgent must implement. Essential for understanding agent architecture, builder pattern, and tool integration.
key_requirements: Agent trait with run() and run_with_handler() methods
AnalystAgent and CodingAgent implementations
Agent execution loop: context manager, LLM completion, tool execution, approval flow
Agent builders with configuration support

specs.reasoning: Shows module dependencies and how agents integrate with LLM providers, tools, storage, security, and context management. Critical for understanding where RestructureCodebaseAgent fits in the cclab-agent system.
key_requirements: Module dependency graph: agents depend on tools, security, context, storage
C4 container diagram showing integration with external APIs
Class diagram showing Agent trait and its relationships with LLMProvider, ToolRegistry, SecurityPolicy, ContextManager

specs.reasoning: Defines fundamental message types, roles, tool calls, and token usage tracking. RestructureCodebaseAgent will use these types for LLM interaction and message history.
key_requirements: Message schema with role, content, tool_calls, timestamp
ToolCall and ToolResult types for tool invocation
TokenUsage type for tracking prompt/completion/total tokens
Role enum: system, user, assistant, tool

specs.reasoning: Describes ContextManager for token budget tracking and message window management. Directly addresses pre-clarification Q1 (30K token budget per group) and message compression strategies.
key_requirements: ContextManager tracks estimated_tokens, max_tokens, available_tokens
Token estimation formula: content.len() / 4
Compression flow: keep system prompt, remove oldest messages when over budget
ContextStats interface for querying context state
Preset defaults: 128k, 32k token limits

specs.reasoning: Defines LLMProvider trait for communicating with external LLMs. RestructureCodebaseAgent must use an LLMProvider to analyze codebases and generate groupings.
key_requirements: LLMProvider trait: provider_name(), supported_models(), validate_model(), complete(), complete_stream()
CompletionRequest schema with messages, model, temperature, max_tokens, tools
CompletionResponse with content, tool_calls, finish_reason, usage
Supported models from Claude, OpenAI, Gemini providers

specs.reasoning: Describes Tool trait, ToolRegistry, and ToolExecutor. RestructureCodebaseAgent may use tools for codebase analysis and group validation during implementation.
key_requirements: Tool trait: name(), description(), parameters(), execute(), definition(), validate_arguments()
ToolRegistry: register, unregister, get, contains, tool_names
ToolExecutor with timeout and retry logic
ToolParameter schema with type validation

specs.reasoning: Describes file reading, glob, grep, and bash tools. May be used during implementation for validating manifest files (pyproject.toml, package.json, Cargo.toml) and analyzing codebase structure.
key_requirements: ReadFileTool for reading manifest files (offset, limit, max 10MB)
GlobTool for finding files matching patterns
GrepTool for searching code by regex
BashTool for executing commands with timeout and output truncation

specs.reasoning: Defines NovaError classification for all error types in agent framework. RestructureCodebaseAgent must properly handle and classify errors during execution.
key_requirements: NovaError variants: LLMError, ToolError, ApprovalRequired, SecurityViolation, ContextOverflow, CommandFailed, etc.
Error classification: is_retriable() vs requires_user_action()
NovaResult type wrapper for agent functions

specs.reasoning: Describes SecurityPolicy and ApprovalHandler for safe tool execution. Framework context for understanding execution constraints and approval flow.
key_requirements: SecurityPolicy: blocked_paths, allowed_paths, blocked_commands, tools_requiring_approval
ApprovalRequest/Response types for tool execution approval
Path validation and command filtering logic
Tool approval flow integration with agents

specs.reasoning: Describes StreamEvent types and StreamHandler for async execution. Framework context for potential streaming implementation of agent execution.
key_requirements: StreamEvent schema: started, thinking, text_chunk, tool_call_requested, tool_execution_*, approval_requested, completed, error
Event flow state machine
StreamHandler implementations: NoOpHandler, PrintHandler, CollectingHandler, CallbackHandler

specs.reasoning: Describes analysis and discovery tools like AskUserTool, TakeNoteTool, RecordFindingTool. May be relevant for user clarification during group generation if needed.
key_requirements: AskUserTool for pausing execution and requesting user input
TakeNoteTool and RecordFindingTool for capturing insights
WebSearchTool and WebFetchTool for external research
PostCommentTool for asynchronous communication

specs.reasoning: Describes a different agent focused on spec context discovery. Related but distinct from RestructureCodebaseAgent's codebase grouping responsibilities. Useful for understanding agent pattern.
key_requirements: Reference context generation pattern with SpecStore search and read
Relevance scoring: high/medium/low
CRR cycle integration with ReviewAgent
Contradiction detection in specifications

specs.reasoning: Describes GitHub/GitLab/Jira platform integrations. Not directly relevant to codebase grouping logic, but provides context on integration patterns used in cclab-agent.
key_requirements: PlatformIntegration trait for issue and comment APIs
Issue schema with state, labels, comments
GitHub, GitLab, Jira OpenAPI mappings

specs.reasoning: Describes session state storage for AnalystAgent. Not directly needed for RestructureCodebaseAgent's grouping logic, but useful context on agent state persistence patterns.
key_requirements: Storage trait: save_session, load_session, list_sessions, delete_session
SessionState schema with messages, notes, findings
MemoryStorage and FileStorage implementations

specs.reasoning: High-level overview of cclab-agent framework. Useful as reference table of all specs and their purposes.
key_requirements: Spec organization: interfaces, core types, agents, tools, storage, security
Reference to all available specification files

gaps: PyO3 bindings spec not found - agent must be exposed via PyO3 per pre-clarification Q6
Manifest parsing spec not found - need to define support for pyproject.toml, package.json, Cargo.toml
Grouping algorithm spec not found - drill-down termination logic and token estimation need detailed design
